sense.definition: a variation of hockey played on inline skates rather than ice skates

examples.x: They practiced inline hockey in the parking lot.
His favorite sport to play is inline hockey.
Inline hockey is played on a smooth surface with inline skates.
Inline hockey is similar to ice hockey but played on wheels.
The team won the inline hockey championship.

sense.definition: a sport adapted from hockey for athletes with physical disabilities, played using sledges

examples.x: Para ice hockey allows athletes with physical disabilities to compete on the ice.
He's been playing para ice hockey since he was a teenager.
The para ice hockey team won gold at the Paralympic Games.
She's a skilled player in para ice hockey.
She's determined to represent her country in para ice hockey at the next Paralympics.

sense.definition: a goal scored in hockey when the opposing team pulls their goalkeeper from ice and replaces them with an extra skater, leaving the net undefended

examples.x: With just seconds remaining on the clock, the forward intercepted a pass and sent the puck sailing into the empty net for an easy empty-netter.
The crowd erupted in cheers as the defenseman launched the puck down the ice and into the open goal, scoring the empty-netter that secured their team's lead.
It was a risky move to pull the goalie so early in the game, and it backfired when the opposing team capitalized with an empty-netter, widening their lead.

sense.definition: a quick and accurate shot in hockey made by flicking the puck with a quick motion of the wrist

examples.x: He scored with a precise wrist shot from the slot.
She practiced her wrist shot technique every day.
His wrist shot had incredible speed and accuracy.
The player's wrist shot beat the goalie top shelf.
She lifted the puck with a perfect wrist shot.

sense.definition: a powerful hockey shot using a swinging motion to strike the puck with force and speed

examples.x: The player scored a goal with a powerful slapshot from the blue line.
His slapshot sent the puck flying into the top corner of the net.
The coach praised the player's slapshot during practice.
The team's victory was sealed by a decisive slapshot in overtime.
The player's slapshot was so fast that it caught the goalie off guard.

sense.definition: a deceptive move in hockey used by a player to fake out an opponent, typically involving quick and agile puck-handling skills

examples.x: She used a deke to create space for a shot on goal.
His deke fooled the opponent and led to a breakaway.
The goalie was left stranded after the player's deke.
The player's deke opened up an opportunity for a pass.
The deke left the goalie guessing and vulnerable.

sense.definition: a penalty in hockey that is called when a player forcefully pushes an opponent into the boards surrounding the rink

examples.x: The referee penalized him for boarding after he shoved the player into the boards.
Boarding is a serious offense in hockey, often resulting in penalties.
He was ejected from the game for a dangerous boarding incident.
Boarding can cause serious injuries to players.

sense.definition: a situation in hockey where one team has a numerical advantage due to a penalty against the opposing team

examples.x: The team scored two goals during their power play.
His team struggled to defend against the opponent's strong power play.
Power plays require quick passing and strategic positioning.
They capitalized on the power play with a well-executed play.
The penalty kill unit successfully defended against the power play.

sense.definition: (hockey) the act of using one's stick or body to cause an opponent to fall or stumble

examples.x: Tripping is against the rules in hockey.
The referee called a tripping penalty on the defenseman.
She apologized to the other player after the tripping incident.

sense.definition: (hockey) the legal act of using physical contact to impede an opponent's progress or separate them from the puck

examples.x: Body checking is a fundamental aspect of defensive play in hockey.
Coaches emphasize the importance of clean body checking in hockey.
She's working on improving her body checking in practice.
Body checking requires good timing and technique.
The player received a penalty for an illegal body checking.

sense.definition: (hockey) the strategy of aggressively pressuring the opposing team in their defensive zone to disrupt their play and regain possession of the puck

examples.x: The coach emphasizes the importance of strong forechecking.

sense.definition: the defensive tactic in hockey where a player rushes back to their own defensive zone to disrupt the opposing team's attack

examples.x: His backchecking broke up the opponent's scoring chance.
Backchecking requires speed and anticipation.
She showed strong backchecking throughout the game.
They practiced backchecking drills during training.
Backchecking helped prevent a goal against our team.

sense.definition: the situation in hockey when a team is playing shorthanded due to a penalty and is defending against the opposing team's power play

examples.x: The team's penalty kill unit is on the ice.
They successfully completed the penalty kill.
Their penalty kill strategy is strong.
She blocked a shot during the penalty kill.
The goalie made several saves on the penalty kill.

sense.definition: one of the two lines that mark the beginning of the offensive and defensive zones on the hockey rink

examples.x: The player skated past the blue line to enter the opponent's zone.
The puck must fully cross the blue line for it to be considered offside.
He made a precise pass from behind the blue line.
The defenseman intercepted the puck at the blue line.
He managed to keep the puck in play at the blue line.

sense.definition: (in ice hockey) to use one's body or stick to block or hinder an opponent's progress or play

examples.x: He used his stick to check the opponent's advance and regain possession of the puck.
She used her body to check the opponent off balance and gain control of the puck.
The forward tried to check the opposing player along the boards.

sense.definition: (hockey) relating to a situation in ice hockey where a player has a clear path to the opponent's goal without any defenders

examples.x: He scored on a breakaway goal in overtime to win the game.
The team capitalized on a breakaway opportunity early in the first period.
The goalie made a spectacular save on a breakaway shot.
She executed a flawless breakaway move to evade the defenseman.
A breakaway chance like that is rare in such a tightly contested match.

sense.definition: a professional ice hockey league comprising teams from North America

examples.x: National Hockey League referees enforce the rules strictly to maintain fair play.
Many National Hockey League players come from diverse backgrounds and countries.

sense.definition: the way play restarts after a stoppage, where two players try to get the puck by using their sticks to hit or guide it to their teammates

examples.x: The referee dropped the puck for the face-off at center ice.
His team won the face-off and quickly moved into the offensive zone.
Face-offs are crucial for starting attacks and gaining possession.
Face-offs happen often during hockey games to restart play.
The center forward was skilled at winning face-offs.

sense.definition: (in hockey) to use one's stick to impede or trip an opponent

examples.x: The forward narrowly avoided a breakaway when the defender attempted to hook him from behind.
The referee's whistle blew as the player was caught hooking the opponent's stick during a scoring chance.
The referee called a penalty when the player hooked the opponent's skate, causing them to lose balance and fall.
The referee penalized the defenseman for hooking the opposing forward as they attempted to drive towards the net.
Despite the referee's warnings, the player continued to hook opponents in an attempt to disrupt their offensive plays.

sense.definition: one of the three equal divisions of play in hockey, each lasting typically 20 minutes

examples.x: Scoring in the opening period set the tone for the rest of the game.

sense.definition: of a play or shot made using the back side of the hockey stick
a rule violation that occurs when a player shoots the puck from behind the center red line across the opposing team's goal line without it being touched by another player
a tiebreaker in hockey where players take turns trying to score on the opposing goalie

examples.x: Our team won the shootout, securing a crucial victory.
A brilliant save by the goalie ended the shootout.
The game ended in a shootout after a 3-3 tie.
She practiced her shootout moves all week.
The team's shootout strategy paid off in the end.
